SEARCH
Package views

Class HtmlGraphWriter

java.lang.Object
views.HtmlGraphWriter
public class HtmlGraphWriter extends Object
A dynamic view-rendering utility that compiles a logical graph topology into an interactive HTML visualization.

The renderer acts as the View compiler in the server's MVC architecture. It translates a logical Graph model into raw node and edge JSON structures, searches standard filesystem locations to locate a static template file (graph.html), and injects the JSON arrays into pre-defined template placeholders (${NODES_DATA} and ${EDGES_DATA}).

Real-Time View Features

  • Identity Suffix Stripping: To support visual simplicity and prevent identical agent classes from overlapping on the canvas, agents are registered with unique memory identity suffixes (e.g., "APlusAgent_182942"). The renderer automatically strips out this memory hash code, rendering clean, human-readable labels (e.g., "PlusAgent") on the canvas.
  • Live State Value Annotation: During compilation, the renderer queries the shared value cache from TopicDisplayer.lastTopicValues in real-time. It appends the current values directly to the topic labels (e.g., "A (10.0)"), generating a live-annotated structural network.
  • Fallback Rendering: If the static template resource cannot be resolved locally, the renderer compiles a safe fallback view to protect the pipeline and prevent rendering errors.

Required Core Framework Components (Internal Dependencies):

  • Graph - Logical graph mapping of registered topics and agents.
  • Node - Structural nodes representing individual communication vertices.
  • TopicDisplayer - Peer servlet whose thread-safe value map is read to fetch live states.
See Also:
  • Graph
  • Node
  • TopicDisplayer

  • Constructor Details

    • HtmlGraphWriter

  • Method Details

    • getGraphHTML

      public static List<String> getGraphHTML(Graph graph)
      Transforms a logical Graph instance into visual HTML layout content.

      Compiles JSON representations of vertices and directed edges, loads the target template file, and replaces the placeholders with the compiled data. If the template is missing, a safe fallback message is returned.

      Parameters:
      graph - the logical graph model representing current topic and agent registrations
      Returns:
      a list of lines containing the updated HTML/Javascript view markup
